summary: Iter269 — verified server-side dedupe now applies to OPEN cashier shifts (previous iter268 only closed). Backend pytest 6/6 (test_closing_dedupe.py) + new 4/4 (test_open_shift_dedupe_iter269.py). GET /api/shifts?status=open&cashiers_only=true&branch_id=76f56acc... returns محمد صبحي exactly once with total_sales=2,266,500; inflated 2,286,500 absent. Without cashiers_only both duplicate open shifts are returned (dedupe correctly scoped). DB untouched — db.shifts.count_documents({notes:'dup-repro'})==2. Closed-shift regression still passes. Frontend Reports → إغلاق الصندوق (dates 2026-07-02→2026-07-03) shows 2,266,500 with NO trace of 2,286,500 in DOM.
critical_code_review_comments: shifts_routes.py _dedupe_closed_cashier_shifts: function name is legacy ('closed') but now handles all statuses. Consider renaming to _dedupe_cashier_shifts to reflect scope.
Dedupe helper wrapped in try/except with warning log — safe fallback if any grouping/ordering error occurs; endpoint still returns raw shifts.
Uses branch_day_truth ground truth when single-cashier group — correctly resolves the operator/cashier name mismatch (orders under معتز مهنا, shift under محمد صبحي).
±1% tolerance on sum vs truth preserves legitimately separated consecutive shifts.
seed_data_creation: Existing seed intact — two OPEN shifts (notes='dup-repro') by محمد صبحي on 2026-07-02 in branch 76f56acc..., totals 2,266,500 and 2,286,500. 3 orders under معتز مهنا totaling 2,266,500. Both docs still in db.shifts (count=2). No data created/deleted by testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoint GET /api/shifts?status=open&cashiers_only=true&branch_id=inventory-accounting-11 returns محمد صبحي once=2,266,500. Same query without cashiers_only returns both duplicates. Closed-shift path also deduped. All 4 new tests + 6 legacy dedupe tests pass. Frontend Reports/إغلاق-الصندوق tab shows correct value only (2,266,500) — no inflated 2,286,500 anywhere in DOM. Individual view toggle 'كل شفت بمفرده' not directly clicked in playwright but summary confirms correct data since API is deduped server-side and cache-proof (never SW-cached).
rca of the issue: Iter268 dedupe helper originally filtered candidates to status='closed' only, so the user's OPEN duplicate (نشط) shifts bypassed dedupe. Fix: 'candidates = list(shifts or [])' — no status filter — while still only running when cashiers_only=True. This dedupes open+closed uniformly on the server response (never SW-cached at /api/*), so it works immediately regardless of stale bundled JS. DB is never mutated (endpoint-only filter).
